text: The surgeon prepares frames and places them visibly on the side of the cell wall. The Ruhmeltourniquet technique is used for the design phase. Pressure is released using xylitol without heat, and the perinephric fat is separated along the joint. Internal pressure is managed using multiple V-Lock sutures.

The surgeon completes the internal printing using a v-lock suture technique, followed by the thermal structure process lasting approximately fourteen minutes. An external nephrology procedure is performed using a number one vikril suture and hemolog. The nephron is fully mobilized and placed inside a collection bag. The bronchitis is placed after the left part of the nerve, and the trachea is removed under vision to control possible hemorrhage. The final external image shows the closure of the surgical injury.
